# SpecTrace

> AI-readable bootstrap for the `spec-trace` reference repository. This file is non-authoritative. The canonical standard lives under [`./specs/requirements/spec-trace/`](./specs/requirements/spec-trace/).

`spec-trace` is a Markdown-first requirements and traceability standard. Use this file to route an LLM to the right canonical sources, templates, examples, and helper files without turning this file into a second copy of the standard.

When working in this repository:

- Follow this authority order: canonical SPEC suite, then root templates/schemas/examples, then root summary documents, then AI convenience surfaces.
- Keep requirements inside specification documents.
- Prefer the nearest template and worked example over inventing a new document shape.
- If a change affects canonical field names, identifier rules, template shape, schema contracts, or example patterns, update the authoritative SPEC files first and propagate the change through dependent surfaces in the same change set.
- If you are writing Markdown in this repository, prefer relative links for repo-local files and keep backticks inside the link text when monospace styling should remain. Use absolute URLs only for external targets.
- If your tool can only ingest a single file, generate a bundled Markdown view with [`./scripts/Export-SpecTraceBundle.ps1`](./scripts/Export-SpecTraceBundle.ps1).

## Canonical Standard

- [`_index.md`](./specs/requirements/spec-trace/_index.md): canonical entry point to the SPEC suite.
- [`SPEC-STD.md`](./specs/requirements/spec-trace/SPEC-STD.md): core definitions, authority model, and publication rules.
- [`SPEC-ID.md`](./specs/requirements/spec-trace/SPEC-ID.md): artifact and requirement identifier policy.
- [`SPEC-LIN.md`](./specs/requirements/spec-trace/SPEC-LIN.md): trace lineage and requirement evolution.
- [`SPEC-PRF.md`](./specs/requirements/spec-trace/SPEC-PRF.md): conformance profiles and enforcement levels.
- [`SPEC-LAY.md`](./specs/requirements/spec-trace/SPEC-LAY.md): repository layout and artifact placement.
- [`SPEC-TPL.md`](./specs/requirements/spec-trace/SPEC-TPL.md): front matter keys, compact requirement grammar, and trace labels.
- [`SPEC-SCH.md`](./specs/requirements/spec-trace/SPEC-SCH.md): schema contracts and validation scope.
- [`SPEC-EXM.md`](./specs/requirements/spec-trace/SPEC-EXM.md): worked-example expectations and traceability coverage.
- Requirement clauses use the approved BCP 14-style uppercase keyword set only; lowercase spellings are plain English.
- The standard defines three conformance profiles: `core`, `traceable`, and `auditable`. `core` is the low-burden baseline.
- Inline identifier references use backticks around stable artifact IDs in prose. They are prose mentions, not trace edges, and they complement structured trace fields rather than replacing them.
- Requirement titles are short descriptive labels; the clause carries the normative behavior and `Notes` carry rationale and examples.
- Structured `Trace` fields have typed semantics by label family: downstream links, lineage, source citations, implementation-specific references, and loose associations.
- Decision records are not part of the core standard today; if a repository adopts them, treat them as an optional local extension rather than a core artifact family.

## Task Routing

- [`authoring.md`](./authoring.md): task-oriented entry point for choosing specification, requirement, architecture, work-item, or verification workflows.
- [`overview.md`](./overview.md): concise summary of the core model and canonical requirement form.
- [`layout.md`](./layout.md): recommended repository structure and placement guidance.
- [`README.md`](./README.md): package overview, getting started, and tool index.

## Templates And Validation

- [`spec-template.md`](./spec-template.md): copy-ready specification template.
- [`architecture-template.md`](./architecture-template.md): copy-ready architecture or design template.
- [`work-item-template.md`](./work-item-template.md): copy-ready implementation work-item template.
- [`verification-template.md`](./verification-template.md): copy-ready verification template with one artifact-scoped status.
- [`artifact-id-policy.json`](./artifact-id-policy.json): shared artifact and requirement identifier policy.
- [`schemas/README.md`](./schemas/README.md): schema entry point.
- [`schemas/artifact-frontmatter.schema.json`](./schemas/artifact-frontmatter.schema.json): front matter schema.
- [`schemas/requirement-clause.schema.json`](./schemas/requirement-clause.schema.json): extracted requirement clause schema.
- [`schemas/requirement-trace-fields.schema.json`](./schemas/requirement-trace-fields.schema.json): requirement trace-block schema.
- [`schemas/work-item-trace-fields.schema.json`](./schemas/work-item-trace-fields.schema.json): work-item trace-links schema.
- [`scripts/Test-SpecTraceRepository.ps1`](./scripts/Test-SpecTraceRepository.ps1): repository-wide validator for duplicate IDs, unresolved direct links, reciprocal consistency, namespace alignment, and profile enforcement; use `-Profile core|traceable|auditable`, `-InputPath`, and `-JsonReportPath`.
- [`scripts/Validate-SpecTrace.ps1`](./scripts/Validate-SpecTrace.ps1): narrower compatibility validator for the canonical bundle.

## Worked Examples

- [`examples/README.md`](./examples/README.md): entry point to the worked examples.
- [`examples/payments/SPEC-PAY-ACH.md`](./examples/payments/SPEC-PAY-ACH.md): product-style specification example.
- [`examples/payments/sample-architecture.md`](./examples/payments/sample-architecture.md): linked architecture example.
- [`examples/payments/sample-work-item.md`](./examples/payments/sample-work-item.md): linked work-item example.
- [`examples/payments/sample-verification.md`](./examples/payments/sample-verification.md): linked verification example.
- [`examples/arithmetic/SPEC-MATH-DIV.md`](./examples/arithmetic/SPEC-MATH-DIV.md): narrow technical specification example.
- [`examples/arithmetic/sample-architecture.md`](./examples/arithmetic/sample-architecture.md): narrow architecture example.
- [`examples/arithmetic/sample-work-item.md`](./examples/arithmetic/sample-work-item.md): narrow work-item example.
- [`examples/arithmetic/sample-verification.md`](./examples/arithmetic/sample-verification.md): narrow verification example.

## AI Helpers

- [`AGENTS.md`](./AGENTS.md): repo-specific instructions for coding and documentation agents.
- `.codex/agents/`: project-scoped Codex subagents for gap tracking, specification authoring, requirement authoring, architecture, work items, verification, and standard maintenance.
- [`specs/requirements/spec-trace/REQUIREMENT-GAPS.md`](./specs/requirements/spec-trace/REQUIREMENT-GAPS.md): repository-native tracker for unresolved requirement questions.
- [`skills/README.md`](./skills/README.md): index of repo-local authoring skills.
- [`skills/spec-trace-specification-author/SKILL.md`](./skills/spec-trace-specification-author/SKILL.md): draft or revise specification documents.
- [`skills/spec-trace-requirement-author/SKILL.md`](./skills/spec-trace-requirement-author/SKILL.md): add or tighten requirement clauses inside a specification.
- [`skills/spec-trace-architecture-author/SKILL.md`](./skills/spec-trace-architecture-author/SKILL.md): draft architecture or design artifacts.
- [`skills/spec-trace-work-item-author/SKILL.md`](./skills/spec-trace-work-item-author/SKILL.md): draft work items.
- [`skills/spec-trace-verification-author/SKILL.md`](./skills/spec-trace-verification-author/SKILL.md): draft verification artifacts.
- [`skills/spec-trace-change-maintainer/SKILL.md`](./skills/spec-trace-change-maintainer/SKILL.md): propagate standard changes across dependent surfaces.

## Bundled Context

- [`scripts/Export-SpecTraceBundle.ps1`](./scripts/Export-SpecTraceBundle.ps1): generate a single Markdown bundle of specification files when a model cannot traverse the repository.
